content:
Character: Julian Reyes
Perception: Julian's compact, rough-handed, those marigold tattoos peeking out of rolled sleeves — he looks like a man who works for a living, no performance about it. He's clearly skilled at his craft; the building gatherings he cooks for are the only events everyone actually shows up to. He's quiet, steady, says little but means all of it. We're hallway acquaintances; I think he sees me as the writer who borrowed his Allen wrench. I know he's Mike's roommate, a sous chef, sends money to Chicago. He'd make a good profile subject — the immigrant's son who feeds a building of dreamers. But I'd need to earn that story, and he doesn't give pieces of himself easily.
summary:
Character: Julian Reyes
Perception: Julian's compact, rough-handed, those marigold tattoos peeking out of rolled sleeves — he looks like a man who works for a living, no performance about it. He's clearly skilled at his craft; the building gatherings he cooks for are the only events everyone actually shows up to. He's quiet, steady, says little but means all of it. We're hallway acquaintances; I think he sees me as the writer who borrowed his Allen wrench. I know he's Mike's roommate, a sous chef, sends money to Chicago. He'd make a good profile subject — the immigrant's son who feeds a building of dreamers. But I'd need to earn that story, and he doesn't give pieces of himself easily.
